name: Beef Chunks Cooked in Coconut Milk
recipeIngredient: 2 sticks fresh lemon grass OR 2 tbsp Dried sliced lemon grass
12 fresh/dried curry leaves OR 4 dried bay leaves
8 fresh/dried kaffir lime leaves OR a 3 X 1/4 inch strip of lemon peel
2 lbs Stewing beef, cut into 1-2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
If using fresh galangal, peel and coarsely chop it and set aside. If using dried galangal, combine with 4 tablespoons water. If using fresh lemon grass, remove the straw-like top, leaving about 6 inches of the bottom. Lightly crush the very bottom and set aside. If using dried lemon grass, add to water with ggalangal.
Crumble and break dried red chilies and cinnamon into enough water to cover, adding cloves. Soak spices for 30 mins. Coarsely dice red pepper (discard seeds), chop shallots/onions, ginger, and garlic.
Blend soaked spices, fresh galangal (if using), diced pepper, shallots, ginger, garlic until smooth. Pour in well-stirred coconut milk. Bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 15 mins, stirring periodically.
Add meat and salt; bring back to boil. Lower heat to medium, cover, and cook uncovered for 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Increase heat to medium-high and continue cooking with periodic stirring until sauce thickens and browns and meat is tender (called KALIYO DAGING). If cooked longer, sauce will disappear, resulting in RENDANG DAGING.
